guest: Jesse Itzler, entrepreneur/endurance athlete (sold private jet company to Warren Buffett, co-founded Zico Coconut Water, 29029)

business_ideas.idea: Sponsor Jesse's home sky bridge and brand it 'the My First Million Sky Bridge'

dynamics: Filmed on location at Jesse Itzler's house as a video-first, low-banter format; one host (primarily Sam, with Shaan present but largely silent) shadows Jesse through his personal year-planning ritual (workout, sauna, planning). Jesse drives the entire episode teaching his system; the host is a student/audience rather than a co-equal debater. Notably light on typical MFM cross-talk and business-idea riffing, emotional and personal in tone.
frameworks: 'Getting light' — declutter phone apps, subscriptions, closets, email/texts to zero before planning the new year, for psychological momentum
Year-end 'closing the books' — personal audit via calendar/phone review, written on one page, to see highlights and gaps
Misogi — one big, hard, year-defining goal chosen annually so every year has a signature accomplishment to look back on
'CEO of your life, not just your company' — plan work around life instead of life around work; treat the calendar as your honest autobiography
Handwritten thank-you letters as a networking and relationship tool ('an ink stain in your brain')
learnings: Planning a full year feels overwhelming, so starting with small, fast decluttering tasks (car, desk, inbox) builds momentum before tackling bigger goals.
A calendar functions less as a scheduling tool and more as a permission structure — if something isn't on it, you don't have to give it energy.
Reviewing the past year methodically (photos, calendar, texts) surfaces forgotten highlights that boost gratitude and motivation more than vague year-end reflection.
Committing to one hard, singular 'misogi' goal per year creates a lifetime portfolio of accomplishments rather than a blur of routine years.
Modeling behavior for your kids (they watch what you actually do, like training for a marathon) teaches more than telling them what to value.
Choosing a personal 'north star' person should be about picking traits you actually want (health, family, adventure), not just chasing the most famous or wealthy example (Elon Musk) wholesale.

banger_quotes.quote: We don't lack time, we lack a system.
I've always thought of myself as the CEO of my company, but not the CEO of my life.
The calendar is your most honest autobiography.
Who the [expletive] wants to have an okay year?

numbers_dropped: Jesse canceled $1,300 of monthly subscriptions during his 'getting light' declutter
Jesse aims to write 10-25 handwritten thank-you letters per year
Host reported 815 unread text messages before doing the exercise
notable: Shot as an in-person, on-location vlog-style episode at the guest's house rather than the usual studio conversation, with minimal banter/business-idea content compared to typical episodes.
